claims.summary: A binary artifact-residue check cannot distinguish semantic void from structurally dense expression whose form carries meaning.
claims.disposition: implement a read-only remainder-texture diagnostic that preserves dense no-marker structure as content requiring semantic review

claims.summary: The exact 512-token boundary behaves differently from the 513-token overflow case.
claims.disposition: verify exact boundary fixtures and preserve the existing cleanup output and token-budget behavior

claims.summary: A token profile is not the same evidence as felt depth, pressure, or semantic weight.
claims.disposition: verify that token-profile and felt-pressure records remain separate and cannot imply one another

claims.summary: Residual structure, lexical diversity, semantic-density proxy, and repeated-symbol texture should remain inspectable after known-marker cleanup.
claims.disposition: implement ArtifactRemainderTextureV1 with bounded surface metrics and an explicit non-causal interpretation boundary

claims.summary: Changing cleanup, budget, provider, or dispatch behavior from the new texture evidence would alter live model behavior.
claims.disposition: require explicit Mike/operator Tier 5 approval with replay, canary, abort, and post-change felt review
